## Brand & Style
The design system is engineered for a modern, high-trust HRMS environment. It prioritizes clarity, efficiency, and a sense of institutional reliability while maintaining the approachability expected of contemporary SaaS. The aesthetic is **Corporate / Modern**, leaning heavily on precision and a systematic layout to handle complex employee data and administrative workflows.

The target audience includes HR administrators in fast-paced Indian tech hubs like Bengaluru and Mumbai, and employees who require quick, friction-free access to payroll and leave management. The UI evokes a sense of organized calm through generous whitespace and a disciplined color application.

## Colors
The palette is rooted in a professional **Deep Indigo** primary, used for action-oriented elements and brand identification. Success, Warning, and Danger states utilize high-legibility shades of Green, Amber, and Red respectively, ensuring critical HR status updates (like leave approvals or payroll errors) are immediately recognizable.

The neutral palette utilizes **Slate Grays** to provide a sophisticated, cool-toned foundation that prevents visual fatigue during long periods of administrative work. Backgrounds use a very light slate tint to separate the canvas from white card elements.

## Typography
**Inter** is the sole typeface for this design system, chosen for its exceptional legibility in data-dense interfaces and its neutral, systematic character. 

The type scale is strictly hierarchical. Headlines use slightly tighter letter-spacing and heavier weights to anchor page sections. Body text is optimized for readability with a comfortable 1.5x line height. **Label-sm** is specifically designed for table headers and metadata, utilizing uppercase styling and increased tracking to provide clear distinction from interactive body text.

## Layout & Spacing
The design system employs a disciplined **8px grid** to ensure mathematical harmony across all components. The layout is a hybrid model:
- **Navigation:** A fixed 280px left sidebar houses the primary navigation and the company logo (e.g., "DeltCRM Mumbai").
- **Header:** A top-bar spans the remaining width, containing global search, notifications, and user profile.
- **Content:** A fluid main area with a maximum container width of 1440px to ensure line lengths remain readable on ultra-wide monitors.

Padding within cards and sections should default to **24px (lg)** to provide the requested generous whitespace, while tighter data clusters (like employee list rows) utilize **16px (md)**.

## Elevation & Depth
Depth is conveyed through **Tonal Layering** supplemented by **Ambient Shadows**. 
- **Level 0 (Surface):** The main application background (Slate-50).
- **Level 1 (Card):** White surfaces used for primary content containers. These use a subtle, diffused shadow: `0px 1px 3px 0px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.1), 0px 1px 2px -1px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.1)`.
- **Level 2 (Overlay):** Dropdowns, modals, and popovers. These use a more pronounced shadow to indicate significant elevation: `0px 10px 15px -3px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.1), 0px 4px 6px -4px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.1)`.

Interactive elements like buttons use a very slight shadow on hover to simulate physical "lift."

## Shapes
The shape language is consistently **Rounded**. A base radius of **8px** is used for buttons and input fields, while primary content cards use a more pronounced **12px (rounded-lg)** to soften the professional interface and make the workspace feel more inviting.

Avatars for employees are circular (full-round) to provide a organic counterpoint to the structured grid.

## Components
- **Buttons:** Primary buttons use the Deep Indigo background with white text. Secondary buttons use a Slate-100 background or a Slate-200 border. Transitions should be a subtle 150ms ease.
- **Input Fields:** Use a Slate-300 border that shifts to Deep Indigo on focus. Labels must always be visible (above the field) in **label-md** style.
- **Cards:** Utilize the 12px radius and Level 1 shadow. In an HR context, cards should clearly group related data like "Salary Breakup (INR)" or "Attendance Summary."
- **Chips/Badges:** Used for status indicators (e.g., "Active," "On Leave," "Probation"). These should use low-saturation background tints of the success/warning/danger colors with high-saturation text.
- **Lists & Tables:** Use horizontal dividers in Slate-100. Row height should be a minimum of 56px to ensure touch-friendly targets and enough vertical breathing room for Indian name lengths and multi-line designations.
- **Sidebar Nav:** Uses a dark-mode-influenced or high-contrast treatment to separate navigation from content. Active states are indicated by a 4px vertical Indigo bar on the left edge of the menu item.

screens.title: B4. Employees List
screens.prompt: B4. Employees List. Data table page. Top banner: Amber warning "190 of 200 seats used — 95% of your plan limit. Upgrade plan". Toolbar: Search box, filter dropdowns (Department, Work Type: Office/Field/Hybrid, Status: Active/On Notice/Terminated), "+ Add employee" (primary), and "Import CSV" (secondary) buttons. Table columns: Avatar + Name (e.g. "Arjun Mehta") + Code ("EMP-102"), Department ("Sales"), Designation ("Field Executive"), Work type chip ("Field"), Manager ("Salim Al-Abri"), Face enrolled (green check icon), Device registered (green check icon), Status chip ("Active"), kebab menu. Pagination footer.

screens.title: H2. Live Field Map
screens.prompt: H2. Live Field Map. Full-bleed map view (Muscat) with sidebar. Map shows numbered avatar pins for 12 active field employees; one selected pin opens a popover card: photo, name, "Last ping 2 min ago", battery 64%, speed/idle tag, buttons "View route" and "Call". Sidebar list of field staff with search, status dot (live/stale >15min/offline), last ping time, battery icon. Top bar: department filter, "Show geofences" toggle displaying translucent circles around offices. Design matches Indigo Enterprise HR system.

- **Level 2 (Overlay):** Dropdowns, modals, and popovers. These use a more pronounced shadow to indicate significant elevation: `0px 10px 15px -3px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.1), 0px 4px 6px -4px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.1)`.

Interactive elements like buttons use a very slight shadow on hover to simulate physical "lift."

## Shapes
The shape language is consistently **Rounded**. A base radius of **8px** is used for buttons and input fields, while primary content cards use a more pronounced **12px (rounded-lg)** to soften the professional interface and make the workspace feel more inviting.

Avatars for employees are circular (full-round) to provide a organic counterpoint to the structured grid.

## Components
- **Buttons:** Primary buttons use the Deep Indigo background with white text. Secondary buttons use a Slate-100 background or a Slate-200 border. Transitions should be a subtle 150ms ease.
- **Input Fields:** Use a Slate-300 border that shifts to Deep Indigo on focus. Labels must always be visible (above the field) in **label-md** style.
- **Cards:** Utilize the 12px radius and Level 1 shadow. In an HR context, cards should clearly group related data like "Salary Breakup (INR)" or "Attendance Summary."
- **Chips/Badges:** Used for status indicators (e.g., "Active," "On Leave," "Probation"). These should use low-saturation background tints of the success/warning/danger colors with high-saturation text.
- **Lists & Tables:** Use horizontal dividers in Slate-100. Row height should be a minimum of 56px to ensure touch-friendly targets and enough vertical breathing room for Indian name lengths and multi-line designations.
- **Sidebar Nav:** Uses a dark-mode-influenced or high-contrast treatment to separate navigation from content. Active states are indicated by a 4px vertical Indigo bar on the left edge of the menu item.

screens.title: S1. Platform Dashboard
screens.prompt: S1. Platform Dashboard: Super admin dashboard for a multi-tenant SaaS. Top row: 4 KPI cards — MRR OMR 1,950 (+12%), Active tenants 87, Total employees 12,450, Failed payments 3 (red accent). Middle: line chart "Revenue last 12 months" and donut chart "Tenants by plan (Basic/Pro/Enterprise)". Bottom: two panels side by side — "Recent signups" table (company, plan, date, status chip) and "System health" list with green/amber status dots (API latency, GPS ingestion queue, payment gateway, push delivery). Use Muscat/Oman data.

## Brand & Style
The design system is engineered for a modern, high-trust HRMS environment. It prioritizes clarity, efficiency, and a sense of institutional reliability while maintaining the approachability expected of contemporary SaaS. The aesthetic is **Corporate / Modern**, leaning heavily on precision and a systematic layout to handle complex employee data and administrative workflows.

The target audience includes HR administrators in fast-paced Indian tech hubs like Bengaluru and Mumbai, and employees who require quick, friction-free access to payroll and leave management. The UI evokes a sense of organized calm through generous whitespace and a disciplined color application.

The neutral palette utilizes **Slate Grays** to provide a sophisticated, cool-toned foundation that prevents visual fatigue during long periods of administrative work. Backgrounds use a very light slate tint to separate the canvas from white card elements.

Avatars for employees are circular (full-round) to provide a organic counterpoint to the structured grid.

## Components

- **Input Fields:** Use a Slate-300 border that shifts to Deep Indigo on focus. Labels must always be visible (above the field) in **label-md** style.
- **Cards:** Utilize the 12px radius and Level 1 shadow. In an HR context, cards should clearly group related data like "Salary Breakup (INR)" or "Attendance Summary."

- **Lists & Tables:** Use horizontal dividers in Slate-100. Row height should be a minimum of 56px to ensure touch-friendly targets and enough vertical breathing room for Indian name lengths and multi-line designations.

- **Lists & Tables:** Use horizontal dividers in Slate-100. Row height should be a minimum of 56px to ensure touch-friendly targets and enough vertical breathing room for Indian name lengths and multi-line designations.
- **Sidebar Nav:** Uses a dark-mode-influenced or high-contrast treatment to separate navigation from content. Active states are indicated by a 4px vertical Indigo bar on the left edge of the menu item.

## Brand & Style
The design system is engineered for a modern, high-trust HRMS environment. It prioritizes clarity, efficiency, and a sense of institutional reliability while maintaining the approachability expected of contemporary SaaS. The aesthetic is **Corporate / Modern**, leaning heavily on precision and a systematic layout to handle complex employee data and administrative workflows.

The target audience includes HR administrators in fast-paced Indian tech hubs like Bengaluru and Mumbai, and employees who require quick, friction-free access to payroll and leave management. The UI evokes a sense of organized calm through generous whitespace and a disciplined color application.

## Colors
The palette is rooted in a professional **Deep Indigo** primary, used for action-oriented elements and brand identification. Success, Warning, and Danger states utilize high-legibility shades of Green, Amber, and Red respectively, ensuring critical HR status updates (like leave approvals or payroll errors) are immediately recognizable.

The neutral palette utilizes **Slate Grays** to provide a sophisticated, cool-toned foundation that prevents visual fatigue during long periods of administrative work. Backgrounds use a very light slate tint to separate the canvas from white card elements.

## Typography
**Inter** is the sole typeface for this design system, chosen for its exceptional legibility in data-dense interfaces and its neutral, systematic character. 

The type scale is strictly hierarchical. Headlines use slightly tighter letter-spacing and heavier weights to anchor page sections. Body text is optimized for readability with a comfortable 1.5x line height. **Label-sm** is specifically designed for table headers and metadata, utilizing uppercase styling and increased tracking to provide clear distinction from interactive body text.

## Layout & Spacing
The design system employs a disciplined **8px grid** to ensure mathematical harmony across all components. The layout is a hybrid model:
- **Navigation:** A fixed 280px left sidebar houses the primary navigation and the company logo (e.g., "DeltCRM Mumbai").
- **Header:** A top-bar spans the remaining width, containing global search, notifications, and user profile.
- **Content:** A fluid main area with a maximum container width of 1440px to ensure line lengths remain readable on ultra-wide monitors.

Padding within cards and sections should default to **24px (lg)** to provide the requested generous whitespace, while tighter data clusters (like employee list rows) utilize **16px (md)**.

## Elevation & Depth
Depth is conveyed through **Tonal Layering** supplemented by **Ambient Shadows**. 
- **Level 0 (Surface):** The main application background (Slate-50).
- **Level 1 (Card):** White surfaces used for primary content containers. These use a subtle, diffused shadow: `0px 1px 3px 0px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.1), 0px 1px 2px -1px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.1)`.
- **Level 2 (Overlay):** Dropdowns, modals, and popovers. These use a more pronounced shadow to indicate significant elevation: `0px 10px 15px -3px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.1), 0px 4px 6px -4px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.1)`.

Interactive elements like buttons use a very slight shadow on hover to simulate physical "lift."

## Shapes
The shape language is consistently **Rounded**. A base radius of **8px** is used for buttons and input fields, while primary content cards use a more pronounced **12px (rounded-lg)** to soften the professional interface and make the workspace feel more inviting.

Avatars for employees are circular (full-round) to provide a organic counterpoint to the structured grid.

## Components
- **Buttons:** Primary buttons use the Deep Indigo background with white text. Secondary buttons use a Slate-100 background or a Slate-200 border. Transitions should be a subtle 150ms ease.
- **Input Fields:** Use a Slate-300 border that shifts to Deep Indigo on focus. Labels must always be visible (above the field) in **label-md** style.
- **Cards:** Utilize the 12px radius and Level 1 shadow. In an HR context, cards should clearly group related data like "Salary Breakup (INR)" or "Attendance Summary."
- **Chips/Badges:** Used for status indicators (e.g., "Active," "On Leave," "Probation"). These should use low-saturation background tints of the success/warning/danger colors with high-saturation text.
- **Lists & Tables:** Use horizontal dividers in Slate-100. Row height should be a minimum of 56px to ensure touch-friendly targets and enough vertical breathing room for Indian name lengths and multi-line designations.
- **Sidebar Nav:** Uses a dark-mode-influenced or high-contrast treatment to separate navigation from content. Active states are indicated by a 4px vertical Indigo bar on the left edge of the menu item.

screens.title: H1. HR Dashboard / Live Board
screens.prompt: H1. HR Dashboard / Live Attendance Board. Real-time HR dashboard. Top KPI strip: Present 142, Late 11 (amber), Absent 9 (red), On field 34, On break 6, Not yet in 18. Main: live grid of employee cards (avatar, name, department, status chip Clocked In/On Break/On Field/Absent, check-in time, small location tag "HQ — Indiranagar") with live-updating pulse dot, filter chips by department and status, view toggle grid/list. Right rail: "Needs attention" feed — 3 pending regularizations, 2 security violations, 5 absentee alerts after 10:00 AM grace with "Notify" buttons. Auto-refresh indicator "Live • updated 4s ago". Realistic Indian company sample data (Oman/Muscat context).

The design system is engineered for a modern, high-trust HRMS environment. It prioritizes clarity, efficiency, and a sense of institutional reliability while maintaining the approachability expected of contemporary SaaS. The aesthetic is **Corporate / Modern**, leaning heavily on precision and a systematic layout to handle complex employee data and administrative workflows.

The target audience includes HR administrators in fast-paced Indian tech hubs like Bengaluru and Mumbai, and employees who require quick, friction-free access to payroll and leave management. The UI evokes a sense of organized calm through generous whitespace and a disciplined color application.

## Colors
The palette is rooted in a professional **Deep Indigo** primary, used for action-oriented elements and brand identification. Success, Warning, and Danger states utilize high-legibility shades of Green, Amber, and Red respectively, ensuring critical HR status updates (like leave approvals or payroll errors) are immediately recognizable.

The neutral palette utilizes **Slate Grays** to provide a sophisticated, cool-toned foundation that prevents visual fatigue during long periods of administrative work. Backgrounds use a very light slate tint to separate the canvas from white card elements.

## Typography
**Inter** is the sole typeface for this design system, chosen for its exceptional legibility in data-dense interfaces and its neutral, systematic character. 

The type scale is strictly hierarchical. Headlines use slightly tighter letter-spacing and heavier weights to anchor page sections. Body text is optimized for readability with a comfortable 1.5x line height. **Label-sm** is specifically designed for table headers and metadata, utilizing uppercase styling and increased tracking to provide clear distinction from interactive body text.

## Layout & Spacing
The design system employs a disciplined **8px grid** to ensure mathematical harmony across all components. The layout is a hybrid model:
- **Navigation:** A fixed 280px left sidebar houses the primary navigation and the company logo (e.g., "DeltCRM Mumbai").
- **Header:** A top-bar spans the remaining width, containing global search, notifications, and user profile.
- **Content:** A fluid main area with a maximum container width of 1440px to ensure line lengths remain readable on ultra-wide monitors.

Padding within cards and sections should default to **24px (lg)** to provide the requested generous whitespace, while tighter data clusters (like employee list rows) utilize **16px (md)**.

## Elevation & Depth
Depth is conveyed through **Tonal Layering** supplemented by **Ambient Shadows**. 
- **Level 0 (Surface):** The main application background (Slate-50).
- **Level 1 (Card):** White surfaces used for primary content containers. These use a subtle, diffused shadow: `0px 1px 3px 0px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.1), 0px 1px 2px -1px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.1)`.
- **Level 2 (Overlay):** Dropdowns, modals, and popovers. These use a more pronounced shadow to indicate significant elevation: `0px 10px 15px -3px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.1), 0px 4px 6px -4px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.1)`.

Interactive elements like buttons use a very slight shadow on hover to simulate physical "lift."

## Shapes
The shape language is consistently **Rounded**. A base radius of **8px** is used for buttons and input fields, while primary content cards use a more pronounced **12px (rounded-lg)** to soften the professional interface and make the workspace feel more inviting.

Avatars for employees are circular (full-round) to provide a organic counterpoint to the structured grid.

## Components
- **Buttons:** Primary buttons use the Deep Indigo background with white text. Secondary buttons use a Slate-100 background or a Slate-200 border. Transitions should be a subtle 150ms ease.
- **Input Fields:** Use a Slate-300 border that shifts to Deep Indigo on focus. Labels must always be visible (above the field) in **label-md** style.
- **Cards:** Utilize the 12px radius and Level 1 shadow. In an HR context, cards should clearly group related data like "Salary Breakup (INR)" or "Attendance Summary."
- **Chips/Badges:** Used for status indicators (e.g., "Active," "On Leave," "Probation"). These should use low-saturation background tints of the success/warning/danger colors with high-saturation text.
- **Lists & Tables:** Use horizontal dividers in Slate-100. Row height should be a minimum of 56px to ensure touch-friendly targets and enough vertical breathing room for Indian name lengths and multi-line designations.
- **Sidebar Nav:** Uses a dark-mode-influenced or high-contrast treatment to separate navigation from content. Active states are indicated by a 4px vertical Indigo bar on the left edge of the menu item.

screens.title: B7. Users & Roles
screens.prompt: B7. Users & Roles. Access control dashboard. Top section: Users table (Avatar + Name, Email, Roles chips like "Business Admin", "HR Manager", Status, Last login, Kebab menu). "Invite user" button opens a side drawer (Email, Role multi-select with descriptions e.g. "HR Manager — operational HR access, no billing"). Bottom section: Roles summary cards (Business Admin: 2 users, HR Manager: 4 users, Team Lead: 12 users, Employee: 190 users). Each card has an "Edit permissions" link. Clean, card-based layout.

screens.title: H16. Muster Roll
screens.prompt: Classic monthly muster roll grid. Sticky first column: employee name + code. Columns: days 1–31 with weekend shading and holiday markers; cells show compact codes with colors: P (green), A (red), HD (amber), L (blue), H (gray), WO (light gray), OD (teal); today column highlighted. Right summary columns: Present days, LOP, OT hours. Toolbar: month switcher, department filter, legend row, "Export for payroll" primary button with lock icon note "exporting locks the month". Indigo enterprise HR style. Wide desktop layout.

screens.title: H10. Attendance Detail
screens.prompt: H10. Employee Attendance Detail (timeline + month). Single-employee attendance page. Header: avatar, name, code, department, policy and shift tags. Left: month mini-calendar with color-coded days (green present, amber half, red absent, blue leave, gray off) and month totals card (worked 176h, late 4 days, OT 6.5h). Right: selected-day vertical timeline — 9:12 Check-in (face ✓ geo ✓, selfie thumbnail, map pin thumbnail), 13:00 Break start, 13:32 Break end, 18:45 Check-out; offline-synced event shows cloud icon with "synced 2h later"; regularized event shows purple "REGULARIZED" tag with approver. Buttons: "Add exception", "Regularize".

The target audience includes HR administrators in fast-paced Indian tech hubs like Bengaluru and Mumbai, and employees who require quick, friction-free access to payroll and leave management. The UI evokes a sense of organized calm through generous whitespace and a disciplined color application.

## Layout & Spacing
The design system employs a disciplined **8px grid** to ensure mathematical harmony across all components. The layout is a hybrid model:
- **Navigation:** A fixed 280px left sidebar houses the primary navigation and the company logo (e.g., "DeltCRM Mumbai").
- **Header:** A top-bar spans the remaining width, containing global search, notifications, and user profile.
- **Content:** A fluid main area with a maximum container width of 1440px to ensure line lengths remain readable on ultra-wide monitors.

Padding within cards and sections should default to **24px (lg)** to provide the requested generous whitespace, while tighter data clusters (like employee list rows) utilize **16px (md)**.

## Elevation & Depth
Depth is conveyed through **Tonal Layering** supplemented by **Ambient Shadows**. 
- **Level 0 (Surface):** The main application background (Slate-50).
- **Level 1 (Card):** White surfaces used for primary content containers. These use a subtle, diffused shadow: `0px 1px 3px 0px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.1), 0px 1px 2px -1px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.1)`.
- **Level 2 (Overlay):** Dropdowns, modals, and popovers. These use a more pronounced shadow to indicate significant elevation: `0px 10px 15px -3px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.1), 0px 4px 6px -4px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.1)`.

Interactive elements like buttons use a very slight shadow on hover to simulate physical "lift."

## Components
- **Buttons:** Primary buttons use the Deep Indigo background with white text. Secondary buttons use a Slate-100 background or a Slate-200 border. Transitions should be a subtle 150ms ease.
- **Input Fields:** Use a Slate-300 border that shifts to Deep Indigo on focus. Labels must always be visible (above the field) in **label-md** style.
- **Cards:** Utilize the 12px radius and Level 1 shadow. In an HR context, cards should clearly group related data like "Salary Breakup (INR)" or "Attendance Summary."
- **Chips/Badges:** Used for status indicators (e.g., "Active," "On Leave," "Probation"). These should use low-saturation background tints of the success/warning/danger colors with high-saturation text.
- **Lists & Tables:** Use horizontal dividers in Slate-100. Row height should be a minimum of 56px to ensure touch-friendly targets and enough vertical breathing room for Indian name lengths and multi-line designations.
- **Sidebar Nav:** Uses a dark-mode-influenced or high-contrast treatment to separate navigation from content. Active states are indicated by a 4px vertical Indigo bar on the left edge of the menu item.

The design system is engineered for a modern, high-trust HRMS environment. screens.title: L2. Apply Leave (Mobile)
screens.prompt: Mobile leave application form for DeltCRM. Header 'Apply for Leave'. 1. 'Select Leave Type' horizontal card selector showing 'Casual' (6.5 left), 'Sick' (4 left), and 'Earned' (12.5 left). 2. 'Select Dates' using a calendar picker highlighting a span from Oct 24 to Oct 26. A note below the calendar reads '3 working days will be deducted (Fri-Sat auto-excluded)'. 3. 'Half-day' toggles for Start and End dates. 4. 'Reason for Leave' textarea with sample text 'Family function in Muscat'. 5. 'Balance After Approval' preview section showing 'Casual Leave: 6.5 → 3.5 days'. Primary CTA button 'Send for Approval' at the bottom. The UI should also have a version for an error state like 'Insufficient Balance' if 10 days were selected.

## Brand & Style
The design system is engineered for a modern, high-trust HRMS environment. It prioritizes clarity, efficiency, and a sense of institutional reliability while maintaining the approachability expected of contemporary SaaS. The aesthetic is **Corporate / Modern**, leaning heavily on precision and a systematic layout to handle complex employee data and administrative workflows.

The target audience includes HR administrators in fast-paced Indian tech hubs like Bengaluru and Mumbai, and employees who require quick, friction-free access to payroll and leave management. The UI evokes a sense of organized calm through generous whitespace and a disciplined color application.

## Colors
The palette is rooted in a professional **Deep Indigo** primary, used for action-oriented elements and brand identification. Success, Warning, and Danger states utilize high-legibility shades of Green, Amber, and Red respectively, ensuring critical HR status updates (like leave approvals or payroll errors) are immediately recognizable.

The neutral palette utilizes **Slate Grays** to provide a sophisticated, cool-toned foundation that prevents visual fatigue during long periods of administrative work. Backgrounds use a very light slate tint to separate the canvas from white card elements.

## Typography
**Inter** is the sole typeface for this design system, chosen for its exceptional legibility in data-dense interfaces and its neutral, systematic character. 

The type scale is strictly hierarchical. Headlines use slightly tighter letter-spacing and heavier weights to anchor page sections. Body text is optimized for readability with a comfortable 1.5x line height. **Label-sm** is specifically designed for table headers and metadata, utilizing uppercase styling and increased tracking to provide clear distinction from interactive body text.

## Layout & Spacing
The design system employs a disciplined **8px grid** to ensure mathematical harmony across all components. The layout is a hybrid model:
- **Navigation:** A fixed 280px left sidebar houses the primary navigation and the company logo (e.g., "DeltCRM Mumbai").
- **Header:** A top-bar spans the remaining width, containing global search, notifications, and user profile.
- **Content:** A fluid main area with a maximum container width of 1440px to ensure line lengths remain readable on ultra-wide monitors.

Padding within cards and sections should default to **24px (lg)** to provide the requested generous whitespace, while tighter data clusters (like employee list rows) utilize **16px (md)**.

## Elevation & Depth
Depth is conveyed through **Tonal Layering** supplemented by **Ambient Shadows**. 
- **Level 0 (Surface):** The main application background (Slate-50).
- **Level 1 (Card):** White surfaces used for primary content containers. These use a subtle, diffused shadow: `0px 1px 3px 0px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.1), 0px 1px 2px -1px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.1)`.
- **Level 2 (Overlay):** Dropdowns, modals, and popovers. These use a more pronounced shadow to indicate significant elevation: `0px 10px 15px -3px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.1), 0px 4px 6px -4px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.1)`.

Interactive elements like buttons use a very slight shadow on hover to simulate physical "lift."

## Shapes
The shape language is consistently **Rounded**. A base radius of **8px** is used for buttons and input fields, while primary content cards use a more pronounced **12px (rounded-lg)** to soften the professional interface and make the workspace feel more inviting.

Avatars for employees are circular (full-round) to provide a organic counterpoint to the structured grid.

## Components
- **Buttons:** Primary buttons use the Deep Indigo background with white text. Secondary buttons use a Slate-100 background or a Slate-200 border. Transitions should be a subtle 150ms ease.
- **Input Fields:** Use a Slate-300 border that shifts to Deep Indigo on focus. Labels must always be visible (above the field) in **label-md** style.
- **Cards:** Utilize the 12px radius and Level 1 shadow. In an HR context, cards should clearly group related data like "Salary Breakup (INR)" or "Attendance Summary."
- **Chips/Badges:** Used for status indicators (e.g., "Active," "On Leave," "Probation"). These should use low-saturation background tints of the success/warning/danger colors with high-saturation text.
- **Lists & Tables:** Use horizontal dividers in Slate-100. Row height should be a minimum of 56px to ensure touch-friendly targets and enough vertical breathing room for Indian name lengths and multi-line designations.
- **Sidebar Nav:** Uses a dark-mode-influenced or high-contrast treatment to separate navigation from content. Active states are indicated by a 4px vertical Indigo bar on the left edge of the menu item.

screens.title: H4. Office Locations & Geofences
screens.prompt: H4. Office Locations & Geofence Editor. Geo-fence configuration page. Left: interactive map with a draggable pin on an office and a circular radius overlay with drag handle; radius field "150 m" synced to the circle. Right form: office name "Muscat Hub", address search box, latitude/longitude readouts, radius slider 50–500m, timezone dropdown, "Office network IPs" repeater rows (203.0.113.42/32) with add/remove, assigned-employees count with manage link. Offices list table below with edit/delete. "+ Add office" button. indigo enterprise style.
